paradox.narrative:
Imagine two athletes. The first ran 50,000 km, but almost exclusively on intense sprints — DC fast charging, harsh accelerations, extreme temperatures. The second ran 100,000 km, but on steady jogging — slow AC charging at home, no overexertion.

Who's healthier? The athlete with fewer km but an aggressive lifestyle has prematurely worn-out knees (battery cells). The one with more km but discipline has intact joints.

This is exactly how your EV battery works.
paradox.conclusion: Mileage lies. SoH tells the truth.

items.question: Is it bad to charge to 100% every night?
items.answer:
Not ideal, but not catastrophic. Li-Ion batteries suffer the most chemical stress at extremes — below 10% and above 90%. Daily charging to 100% slightly accelerates degradation, but less than a single DC Fast Charging session at high temperatures.

💡 Golden rule: keep between 20%-80% for daily use. Charge to 100% only before long trips.

items.question: What do I do if the report shows RED (High Risk)?
items.answer:
A RED score doesn't automatically mean 'don't buy'. It means 'don't buy without a second opinion'.

🔍 We recommend:
1. Request a Level 2 Diagnosis (The Surgeon) for live battery data.
2. If confirmed SoH is below 75%, request a physical inspection at an authorized service center.
3. Use the report as a negotiation tool — a SoH of 78% vs. 92% can make a difference of thousands of EUR in price.

items.question: How accurate is the AI Risk Score?
items.answer:
Our score doesn't tell you 'the battery has exactly 87.3% health'. That would be a lie without live data. What our AI does is probabilistic modeling — it analyzes:

• VIN history (accidents, market of origin, recalls)
• Statistical patterns of the specific model (average NMC vs LFP degradation)
• Risk indicators (frequent owner changes, imports from unreliable markets)

It's a risk score, not a live reading. For real SoH, you need Level 2 (The Surgeon).

items.question: Can I send the report to the seller?
items.answer: Yes! Every InspectEV report can be shared directly via WhatsApp or Email with a single tap. The report includes a secure link valid for the report's lifetime (30 days Level 1 / 365 days Level 2). The seller can view the full report without an InspectEV account.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers before you enter your VIN.
faq.a1:
Inspect-EV works as a secure bridge between you and the computer of the electric car you want to inspect. Everything happens 100% online, with no cables, adapters, or service visits, in 3 simple steps:

Initial Scan (Free): You enter the chassis number (VIN). Our system instantly checks whether the car supports our Cloud diagnostics and searches international databases for any damage alerts.

Authorization & Live Extraction: Once you choose a package, the system generates a secure link. If the car isn't yours, you send this link to the seller (current owner). They quickly connect with the car's official account (e.g. the Tesla or VW app) to grant us temporary access. We then immediately extract the real battery health (SoH) and mileage directly from the car's 'brain' (BMS), ensuring the data hasn't been tampered with.

The Full Report: We combine the live-extracted battery data with detailed damage and service history (integrated via our data partners), delivering an interactive report and an official PDF.

What are you actually paying for? By purchasing a report, you get instant access to uncorrupted data about the car's most expensive component (the battery) and full visibility into its past. You're paying for your peace of mind and for solid arguments that, more often than not, recoup the report cost directly from the price negotiation.
faq.a2:
The content varies by package, but the goal is the same: to give you an absolute advantage before buying an electric car.

Standard Package (Vehicle History & Battery Prediction):

Complete Damage History: Reports from international databases about accidents, collisions, and hidden repairs.

Mileage & Legal Checks: Official records to detect odometer rollbacks and searches in theft databases.

AI Predictive Degradation: A mathematically calculated graph showing you how the battery of this specific model theoretically degrades over time, compared to the global average.

Premium Package (Cloud Battery Diagnostics + History):
This is our ultimate report. On top of the full vehicle history from the Standard Package, we generate a secure link that the seller accesses to allow us to officially connect to the car. From that moment, we extract and analyze live the following data from the BMS (Battery Management System):

Real Battery Health (SoH): The exact percentage of life remaining in the battery's chemical structure at the time of inspection.

Degraded Capacity (in kWh): How much energy the battery can effectively hold now, versus the day it left the factory.

Estimated Real Range: How many kilometers the car can actually travel on a full charge in real life, not what the manufacturer states in the manual.

Stress Analysis (Charging Pattern): We analyze how the car was 'treated' (whether the battery was abused exclusively at high-speed DC Fast-Charge stations, or whether it was charged correctly, slowly, via AC).

Secured Mileage (BMS Odometer): We read the mileage recorded deep in the motor/battery control unit, a value impossible to tamper with by manipulating the dashboard display.

Regardless of the package chosen, you get access to an interactive Dashboard and an official PDF — your primary tool for negotiating a fair price.
faq.q3: How long until I receive the report?
faq.a3: About 30 seconds for the Standard package — the result is instant once the VIN is validated. For Premium, Smartcar live battery diagnostics adds 1–2 minutes (OAuth authorization in the manufacturer's app + BMS reading).
faq.q4: What's the difference between Standard and Premium?
faq.a4:
The difference lies in the shift from estimation to absolute certainty about the car's most expensive component: the battery (which can represent up to 50% of the vehicle's total value).

The Standard Package is your baseline shield. It's perfect for quickly ruling out a car with a shady past. It gives you the full damage history (via official vehicle history registries) and shows you whether the car was in a serious accident or has rolled-back mileage in official inspection records. Regarding the battery, we only offer a theoretical prediction (how the battery should look at its age, based on an AI algorithm).

The Premium Package is your ultimate safety and negotiation tool. We move from theory to real data, extracted live from the car's computer (BMS). With this report, you can deduce and calculate vital aspects:

Financial Risk & Price Savings: A new battery costs between €10,000 and €20,000. If the Premium report shows a capacity (SoH) degraded by 15–20%, you know exactly that the car is worth much less. Our buyers use this official PDF to negotiate and knock thousands of euros off the seller's asking price, instantly recouping the report cost.

Abuse History (How It Was Driven): From the 'Stress Analysis' you can immediately deduce whether the car was used as a Taxi/Ride-share (abused daily with rapid DC charging that 'fries' the cells) or whether it had a careful owner who charged it slowly at home (AC).

Real Range Calculation: The seller will promise you the factory range (e.g. 400 km). The Premium report shows you exactly how many kWh the battery can store today. This way, you can clearly calculate that its real range is 330 km (and even less in winter), helping you decide whether the car can handle your daily commute without unpleasant surprises after purchase.

In short: The Standard Package ensures you don't buy a hidden wreck. The Premium Package shows you the exact real technical condition of the battery and gives you the mathematical weapons needed to negotiate a fair price.
faq.q5: Where do I find the VIN?
faq.a5: The VIN has 17 characters and can be found in 3 places: on the dashboard (bottom-left corner of the windshield, visible from outside), in the vehicle registration document, and on the driver's door jamb. It never contains the letters I, O or Q to avoid confusion with the digits 1 and 0.
faq.q6: Can I get a refund if I'm not satisfied?
faq.a6: Yes — we offer a 14-day guarantee for inaccurate or incomplete reports, per our [[full refund policy|/legal/refund]]. If the decoded data is wrong or important sections are missing, we refund the full amount paid.
